description: LEFT JOIN with multiple columns from right side all become nullable

columns.name: order_id
columns.note: RIGHT side of LEFT JOIN: becomes nullable

columns.name: total
columns.note: RIGHT side of LEFT JOIN: becomes nullable

columns.name: notes
columns.note: Already nullable in schema AND on right side of LEFT JOIN

columns.name: order_date
columns.note: RIGHT side of LEFT JOIN: becomes nullable

name: left_join_multiple_cols
query_sql:
-- @name GetUsersWithOrderDetails
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.id, u.name, o.id AS order_id, o.total, o.notes, o.created_at AS order_date FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks all right-side columns as nullable due to LEFT JOIN semantics
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks order_id, total, and order_date as non-nullable based on schema definitions
